methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $6.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Tempus AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $6B+

private.name: Freenome
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $2.6B

private.name: Alto Neuroscience
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Public), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Recursion Pharmaceuticals is a public-stage biotech company in the Biotech & Health sector, currently valued at $1.8B following $1B+ in cumulative funding. The peer universe includes 3 public comparables (Tempus AI, Freenome, Alto Neuroscience) and 8 private peers spanning the same sector and stage, providing a relevant benchmark set for valuation triangulation.

The peer-median method was the primary applicable approach given data constraints. Using 3 same-sector, same-stage public comparables, the median valuation across the peer set stands at $2.6B, with a range spanning $529M (Alto Neuroscience) to $6.0B (Tempus AI). The public-comp anchoring method, which would apply the sector's 5.8x P/S multiple, could not be deployed due to absence of revenue data on file. Revenue disclosure remains sparse for private biotech firms, rendering this approach inapplicable for this target.

The peer-median analysis supports a valuation range of $2.2B to $3.0B, centered on $2.6B. This implies approximately 44% upside from the current $1.8B valuation. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (11x range from floor to ceiling), which reflects stage-dependent risk heterogeneity across early clinical, late-stage, and commercial-ready biotech programs. Revenue multiples remain unavailable, preventing cross-validation via income-based methods. The analysis rests on a single triangulation method and warrants sensitivity analysis around peer selection and comparable stage proximity.
